3188-michael-burnham-meets-adiatya-sahil.note: Memory Alpha spells the name 'Adiatya' in this bullet and 'Aditya' in its own 3148 entry. The authored summary uses the correct spelling, so the token is absent from this source line by design.

2362-he-later-operates-a-field-transport.note: This bullet's subject is only 'He' in the source, a pronoun continuing from the immediately preceding sub-bullet on the same year page ('A Federation soldier, Miles O'Brien, kills a Cardassian soldier during the massacre... He later operates a field transporter...'), both grouped under the USS Rutledge heading. Confirmed against data/events.raw.json. Naming O'Brien directly is a correct read of the source, not a fabrication.

2363-eight-months-before-her-destruction.note: This bullet's subject is only 'this ship' in the source, referring back to the K.E. Tsiolkovsky named in the immediately preceding sub-bullet under the same {{SS|Tsiolkovsky}} heading on the 2363 year page. Confirmed against data/events.raw.json.

2362-the-oshc-investigation-into-finn-s-.note: Stardate 40999.4 is 0.4 over the 2362 sidebar's declared 39355-40999 range. Memory Alpha's own 'Background information' section for this year documents the inconsistency directly: the {{ste|c}} guideline says 2362 should run 39000-39999, but two in-universe sources cite stardates in the 40000s that year, so the page's own declared range is already known to undershoot its cited events by design, not by parser or authoring error.

2363-the-enterprise-is-commissioned-at-t.note: Stardate 40759.5 is 0.5 over the 2363 sidebar's declared 40052-40759 range. The sidebar range is built from the same year's whole-number stardate citations (40052 and 40759.5 itself, truncated), so the .5 fraction on the upper bound's own source event pushes it just past the rounded range boundary. A rounding artifact in Memory Alpha's sidebar, not an authoring error.

2366-six-months-before-alexana-devos-is-.note: This sub-bullet's own text never names the planet - it inherits 'Rutia IV' from the parent bullet directly above it ('On Rutia IV, these events occurred prior to stardate 43510:'), which is itself a separate dataset event. Confirmed against data/events.raw.json. Same pronoun/antecedent pattern as the two exceptions above, but for a location instead of a name.

2366-two-months-before-a-cell-of-the-ans.note: Same as the Alexana Devos entry immediately above: this sub-bullet inherits 'Rutia IV' from the parent bullet ('On Rutia IV, these events occurred prior to stardate 43510:') rather than naming it directly. Confirmed against data/events.raw.json.

2379-yor-travels-from-this-year-to-the-f.note: The word 'Kelvin' doesn't appear in this bullet's own text, but its 'this year' link targets 'Narada incursion reality' and the bullet sits under this year's '=== Alternate reality ===' heading - Memory Alpha's own structural signal for the Kelvin timeline (see CLAUDE.md on 'alternate reality' as MA's Kelvin idiom). Naming the Kelvin timeline directly states what the section heading and link already establish, not an addition.

2381-engages-in-a-chase-with-the-cerrito.note: This bullet's own text never names its subject ('Engages in a chase with the Cerritos...'), inheriting it from the ';{{USS|Aledo}}' subheading it's grouped under on the 2381 year page, alongside the Aledo's other bullets in the same block. Confirmed against data/events.raw.json.

2381-engages-in-a-second-contact-race-wi.note: This bullet's own text never names its subject ("Engages in a second contact race with the USS Aledo..."), inheriting it from the USS Cerritos block it's grouped under on the 2381 year page - the immediately preceding bullets there are about Vice Admiral Les Buenamigo tricking the Cerritos into an encounter with the Breen, and Starfleet's Shame airing for the Cerritos crew. Confirmed against data/events.raw.json.

2381-in-later-battled-a-pakled-clumpship.note: This bullet's own text never names its subject ("In later battled a Pakled Clumpship..."), inheriting it from the ';{{USS|Titan|NCC-80102}}' subheading it's grouped under on the 2381 year page, alongside the Titan's other bullets in the same block. Confirmed against data/events.raw.json.

2381-it-continued-to-battle-the-pakleds-.note: Same USS Titan block as the entry above: this bullet's subject is only 'It' in the source, continuing from the Titan bullets directly preceding it under the same subheading. Confirmed against data/events.raw.json.

2381-lures-the-uss-aledo-uss-dallas-and-.note: This bullet's own text never names its subject ("Lures the USS Aledo, USS Dallas, and USS Corpus Christi..."), inheriting it from the USS Cerritos block it's grouped under on the 2381 year page - it directly follows the 'Engages in a second contact race' Cerritos bullet above. Confirmed against data/events.raw.json.

2381-takes-part-in-a-second-contact-race.note: This bullet's own text never names its subject ("Takes part in a second contact race with the Cerritos..."), inheriting it from the ';{{USS|Aledo}}' subheading it's grouped under on the 2381 year page. Confirmed against data/events.raw.json.

2381-the-ship-is-called-in-to-help-media.note: This bullet's subject is only 'The ship' in the source, grouped under the USS Cerritos block on the 2381 year page directly after bullets about Captain Freeman and the Cerritos crew. Confirmed against data/events.raw.json.

2381-the-station-plays-host-to-post-war-.note: This bullet's subject is only 'The station' in the source, grouped under the ';[[Deep Space 9]]' subheading it opens the 2381 year page's events with. Confirmed against data/events.raw.json.

2384-the-voyager-is-launched-on-her-maid.note: This bullet's own text just says "The Voyager", but it's grouped under the ';{{USS|Voyager|NCC-74656-A|-A}}' subheading on the 2384 year page - the successor ship, not the original Voyager. Naming it 'the USS Voyager-A' states what the section heading already establishes. Confirmed against data/events.raw.json.

2385-in-the-aftermath-of-this-attack-sta.note: This bullet's own text only says 'this attack', referring back to the 'attack on Mars' bullet directly above it on the 2385 year page (the April 5 synthetic-triggered attack on Utopia Planitia). Confirmed against data/events.raw.json.

2385-starfleet-abandons-its-plans-to-aid.note: Same antecedent as the entry above: this bullet's 'even before the attack' clause refers to the 'attack on Mars' bullet earlier on the 2385 year page. Confirmed against data/events.raw.json.

2390-after-being-confronted-by-his-first.note: This bullet's own text names its subject only as 'Vandermeer', with his rank and first name established in the immediately preceding sibling bullet ('Captain Alonzo Vandermeer of the USS Ibn Majid receives a black flag directive...') under the same top-level events list on the 2390 year page. Confirmed against data/events.raw.json.

2404-by-this-year-the-doctor-had-named-h.note: This bullet's own text is just 'By this year, The Doctor had named himself "Joe"...', with no mention of Voyager, the Borg, Earth, or 2378 - those all come from the '*' intro line it's nested under as a '**' sub-bullet on the 2404 year page ('In an alternate timeline in which the USS Voyager does not enter the Borg transwarp conduit network and return to Earth in 2378:'). Same nested-bullet pattern recorded in data/timeline-overrides.json for this event's timeline field; see that entry for why it's confirmed a one-off rather than a parser change.
